chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and keep it washed off after complexplate surgery.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Transcription byumen's official twitter account,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: where not everything is wouldn't be wild.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: anozovalk,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: H Одай Совшенnelle А в亮овbleим

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: fishermen are super hot as we eat verified products.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Follow the worst Today's worst news

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Watch the worst nowadays

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and Людмай Лар receptive Therefore life won't slow.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: supply

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and supply

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: yet spare

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: For black men, the barbershop is not just a place

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: where you get your hair cut or your beard trimmed.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: No, it's much more than that.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Historically, the barbershop has been a safe haven for black men.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: It's a place where we go for friendship,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: solidarity and solace.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: It's a place where we go to get away from the stress

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: of the grind of work.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: And sometimes home life.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: It's a place where we don't have to worry

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: about how we're being perceived by the outside world.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: It's a place where we don't feel threatened

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: or threatening.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: It's a place of loyalty and trust.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: For that reason,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: it's one of the few places where we can fearlessly be ourselves

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and just talk.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The talk, the shop talk, the conversation,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: that is the essence of the black barbershop.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: I can remember going to the barbershop with my dad as a kid.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: We went to Mr. Mike's barbershop every other Saturday.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: And like clockwork,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: the same group of men would be there every time we went,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: either waiting on their favorite barber

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: or just soaking up the atmosphere.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: I can remember the jovial greeting

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: that warmly welcomed us every time we went.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Hey, Rev!

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: They would say to my dad.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: He's a local pastor, and they treated him like a celebrity.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Hey, young fella, how you doing?

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: They would say to me,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: making me feel just as special.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: I remember the range of the conversations was immense.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The men would talk about politics and sports and music

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and world news, national news,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: neighborhood news.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: There was some talk about women

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and what it was like to be a black man in America.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: But many times, they also talked about health.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The conversations about health were lengthy and deep.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The men often recounted their doctor's recommendations

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to cut salt in their diet

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: or to eat less fried foods

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: or to stop smoking

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: or to reduce stress.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: They talked about the different ways you could reduce stress,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: like simplifying one's love life.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: (*Laughter*)

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: All ways to treat high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: There's a lot of talk about high blood pressure in the barbershop.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: That's because almost 40 percent of black men have it.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: That means that almost every single black man

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: either has high blood pressure or knows a black man who has it.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Sometimes, those conversations in the barbershop

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: would be about what happens

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: when high blood pressure is not adequately addressed.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Say, did you hear about Jimmy?

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: He had a stroke.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: He had a stroke.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Did you hear about Eddie?

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: He died last week.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Massive heart attack.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: He was 50.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: More black men die from high blood pressure than from anything else,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: even though decades of medical wisdom and science

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: have demonstrated that death from high blood pressure

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: can be prevented with timely diagnosis and appropriate treatment.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: So why is high blood pressure so differentially deadly for black men?

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Because too often, high blood pressure is either untreated

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: or undertreated in black men,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: in part because of our lower engagement with the primary health care system.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Black men, in particular those with high blood pressure,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: are less likely to have a primary care doctor than other groups.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: But why?

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: But why?

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Some of our earliest research on black men's health

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: revealed that for many,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: the doctor's office is associated with fear,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: mistrust,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: disrespect

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and unnecessary unpleasantness.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The doctor's office is only a place that you go when you don't feel well,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and when you do go,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: you might wait for hours,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: only to get the runaround

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and to be evaluated by a stoic figure in a white coat

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: who only has 10 minutes to give you

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and who doesn't value the talk.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: So it's no wonder that some men don't want to be bothered

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and skip going to the doctor altogether,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: especially if they feel fine.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: But herein lays the problem.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: You can feel just fine

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: while high blood pressure ravages your most vital organs.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: This is Denny Moe,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: owner of Denny Moe's Superstar Barber Shop in Harlem.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: I've been lucky enough to have Denny as my barber for the last eight years.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: He said to me once,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Hey, doc, you know,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: lots of black men trust their barbers more than they trust their doctors.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: This was stunning to me.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: At first,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: but not so much when you think about it.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Black men have been with their current barbers,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: on average, as long as I've been with Denny,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: about eight years.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: And black men see their barbers about every two weeks.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Not only do you trust your barber with your look and with your style,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: but you also trust him with your secrets

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and sometimes your life.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Denny, like many barbers,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: is more than just an artist, a businessman and confidant.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: He's a leader and a passionate advocate for the well-being of his community.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The very first time I walked into Denny Moe's shop,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: he wasn't just cutting hair.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: He was also orchestrating a voter registration drive

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to give a voice to his customers and his community.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: With this kind of activism

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and community investment that typifies the black barbershop,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: of course the barbershop is a perfect place

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to talk about high blood pressure and other health concerns in the community.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: First, the barbershop is not a medical setting,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and so it doesn't have all of the negative psychological baggage

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: that comes along with that.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: When you're in a barbershop,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: you're not going to be able to talk to your friends

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: when you're in a barbershop.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: You're in your territory,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and you're among friends who share your history,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: your struggle and your health risks.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Second, because the barbershop is a place of connection,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: loyalty and trust,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: it's a place where you're more open to have a conversation about health

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and especially about high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: After all, conversations about high blood pressure

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: have all of the elements of great shop talk.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Stress and high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Food and high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Relationships and high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: And yes, what it's like to be a black man in America

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: But you can do more than just talk about high blood pressure in the barbershop.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: You can concretely take action.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Here we have an opportunity to partner with the Denny Moes of the world

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and empower communities to address the health inequities

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: that uniquely affect it.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: When high blood pressure screening expanded from clinics and hospitals

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to communities in the 1960s and 70s,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: black physicians like Dr. Eli Saunders in Baltimore

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and Dr. Keith Ferdinand in New Orleans

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: were at the forefront of bringing health promotion to community hubs

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: in urban black neighborhoods.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: These pioneers paved the way for my professional journey

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: with barbershops and health,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: which began in Chicago in medical school.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The very first research project that I worked on as a medical student

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: was to help design health care interventions

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: that would appeal to black men.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: We conducted about a dozen focus groups

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: with a broad cross-section of black men,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and we learned that for them,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: being healthy was as much about being perceived as healthy

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: as it was about feeling healthy,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and that feeling good went hand in hand with looking good.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: This work led to the development of Project Brotherhood,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: a community clinic founded by Dr. Eric Whitaker

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: that provided tailored health care to black men.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Part of this tailored care

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: involved having a barber on the premises

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to reward the men who came for needed health care

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: with a free haircut,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to let the men know that we too valued how they looked

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: as well as how they felt,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and that what was important to them was also important to us.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: But while there's only one Project Brotherhood,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: there are thousands of black barbershops

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: that are located in the area.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: And we have a number of black barbershops

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: where the intersection of health and haircuts can be cultivated.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The next stop on my journey was Dallas, Texas,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: where we learned that barbers were not only willing,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: but fully able to roll up their sleeves

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and participate in delivering needed health services

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to improve the health of their customers and their community.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: We also learned about the Black Barbers

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and taught them how to measure blood pressure

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and how to counsel their customers

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and refer them to doctors to help manage high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: The barbers were not only willing to do it,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: but they were damn good at it.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Over a three-year period,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: the barbers measured thousands of blood pressures,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: resulting in hundreds of black men being referred to doctors

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: for medical care of their high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: These doctor partnerships resulted in a 20 percent increase

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: in the number of men who were able to achieve target blood pressure levels,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and a three-point drop, on average,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: in the blood pressure of each participant.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: If we were to extrapolate that three-point drop

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to every single black man with high blood pressure in America,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: we would prevent 800 heart attacks,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: 500 strokes

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and 900 deaths from high blood pressure.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: In just one year.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: And our experience with barbershops has been no different in New York City,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: where my journey has currently led me.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: With an incredible team of diverse research assistants,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: community health workers and volunteers,

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: we've been able to partner with over 200 barbershops

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and other trusted community venues

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to reach over 7,000 older black men.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: And we've offered high blood pressure screening and counseling

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: to each and every one of them.

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: Thanks to Denny Moe

chunks.title: How_barbershops_can_keep_men_healthy
chunks.text: and the myriad other barbers and community leaders

Follow the worst Today's worst news Watch the worst nowadays and Людмай Лар receptive Therefore life won't slow. supply and supply yet spare For black men, the barbershop is not just a place where you get your hair cut or your beard trimmed. No, it's much more than that. Historically, the barbershop has been a safe haven for black men. It's a place where we go for friendship, solidarity and solace. It's a place where we go to get away from the stress of the grind of work. And sometimes home life. It's a place where we don't have to worry about how we're being perceived by the outside world. It's a place where we don't feel threatened or threatening. It's a place of loyalty and trust. For that reason, it's one of the few places where we can fearlessly be ourselves and just talk. The talk, the shop talk, the conversation, that is the essence of the black barbershop. I can remember going to the barbershop with my dad as a kid. We went to Mr. Mike's barbershop every other Saturday. And like clockwork, the same group of men would be there every time we went, either waiting on their favorite barber or just soaking up the atmosphere. I can remember the jovial greeting that warmly welcomed us every time we went. Hey, Rev! They would say to my dad. He's a local pastor, and they treated him like a celebrity. Hey, young fella, how you doing? They would say to me, making me feel just as special. I remember the range of the conversations was immense. The men would talk about politics and sports and music and world news, national news, neighborhood news. There was some talk about women and what it was like to be a black man in America. But many times, they also talked about health. The conversations about health were lengthy and deep. The men often recounted their doctor's recommendations to cut salt in their diet or to eat less fried foods or to stop smoking or to reduce stress. They talked about the different ways you could reduce stress, like simplifying one's love life. (*Laughter*) All ways to treat high blood pressure. There's a lot of talk about high blood pressure in the barbershop. That's because almost 40 percent of black men have it. That means that almost every single black man either has high blood pressure or knows a black man who has it. Sometimes, those conversations in the barbershop would be about what happens when high blood pressure is not adequately addressed. Say, did you hear about Jimmy? He had a stroke. He had a stroke. Did you hear about Eddie? He died last week. Massive heart attack. He was 50. More black men die from high blood pressure than from anything else, even though decades of medical wisdom and science have demonstrated that death from high blood pressure can be prevented with timely diagnosis and appropriate treatment. So why is high blood pressure so differentially deadly for black men? Because too often, high blood pressure is either untreated or undertreated in black men, in part because of our lower engagement with the primary health care system. Black men, in particular those with high blood pressure, are less likely to have a primary care doctor than other groups. But why? But why? Some of our earliest research on black men's health revealed that for many, the doctor's office is associated with fear, mistrust, disrespect and unnecessary unpleasantness. The doctor's office is only a place that you go when you don't feel well, and when you do go, you might wait for hours, only to get the runaround and to be evaluated by a stoic figure in a white coat who only has 10 minutes to give you and who doesn't value the talk. So it's no wonder that some men don't want to be bothered and skip going to the doctor altogether, especially if they feel fine. But herein lays the problem. You can feel just fine while high blood pressure ravages your most vital organs. This is Denny Moe, owner of Denny Moe's Superstar Barber Shop in Harlem. I've been lucky enough to have Denny as my barber for the last eight years. He said to me once, Hey, doc, you know, lots of black men trust their barbers more than they trust their doctors. This was stunning to me. At first, but not so much when you think about it. Black men have been with their current barbers, on average, as long as I've been with Denny, about eight years. And black men see their barbers about every two weeks. Not only do you trust your barber with your look and with your style, but you also trust him with your secrets and sometimes your life. Denny, like many barbers, is more than just an artist, a businessman and confidant. He's a leader and a passionate advocate for the well-being of his community. The very first time I walked into Denny Moe's shop, he wasn't just cutting hair. He was also orchestrating a voter registration drive to give a voice to his customers and his community. With this kind of activism and community investment that typifies the black barbershop, of course the barbershop is a perfect place to talk about high blood pressure and other health concerns in the community. First, the barbershop is not a medical setting, and so it doesn't have all of the negative psychological baggage that comes along with that. When you're in a barbershop, you're not going to be able to talk to your friends when you're in a barbershop. You're in your territory, and you're among friends who share your history, your struggle and your health risks. Second, because the barbershop is a place of connection, loyalty and trust, it's a place where you're more open to have a conversation about health and especially about high blood pressure. After all, conversations about high blood pressure have all of the elements of great shop talk. Stress and high blood pressure. Food and high blood pressure. Relationships and high blood pressure. And yes, what it's like to be a black man in America and high blood pressure. But you can do more than just talk about high blood pressure in the barbershop. You can concretely take action. Here we have an opportunity to partner with the Denny Moes of the world and empower communities to address the health inequities that uniquely affect it. When high blood pressure screening expanded from clinics and hospitals to communities in the 1960s and 70s, black physicians like Dr. Eli Saunders in Baltimore and Dr. Keith Ferdinand in New Orleans were at the forefront of bringing health promotion to community hubs in urban black neighborhoods. These pioneers paved the way for my professional journey with barbershops and health, which began in Chicago in medical school. The very first research project that I worked on as a medical student was to help design health care interventions that would appeal to black men. We conducted about a dozen focus groups with a broad cross-section of black men, and we learned that for them, being healthy was as much about being perceived as healthy as it was about feeling healthy, and that feeling good went hand in hand with looking good. This work led to the development of Project Brotherhood, a community clinic founded by Dr. Eric Whitaker that provided tailored health care to black men. Part of this tailored care involved having a barber on the premises to reward the men who came for needed health care with a free haircut, to let the men know that we too valued how they looked as well as how they felt, and that what was important to them was also important to us. But while there's only one Project Brotherhood, there are thousands of black barbershops that are located in the area. And we have a number of black barbershops where the intersection of health and haircuts can be cultivated. The next stop on my journey was Dallas, Texas, where we learned that barbers were not only willing, but fully able to roll up their sleeves and participate in delivering needed health services to improve the health of their customers and their community. We also learned about the Black Barbers and taught them how to measure blood pressure and how to counsel their customers and refer them to doctors to help manage high blood pressure. The barbers were not only willing to do it, but they were damn good at it. Over a three-year period, the barbers measured thousands of blood pressures, resulting in hundreds of black men being referred to doctors for medical care of their high blood pressure. These doctor partnerships resulted in a 20 percent increase in the number of men who were able to achieve target blood pressure levels, and a three-point drop, on average, in the blood pressure of each participant. If we were to extrapolate that three-point drop to every single black man with high blood pressure in America, we would prevent 800 heart attacks, 500 strokes and 900 deaths from high blood pressure. In just one year. And our experience with barbershops has been no different in New York City, where my journey has currently led me. With an incredible team of diverse research assistants, community health workers and volunteers, we've been able to partner with over 200 barbershops and other trusted community venues to reach over 7,000 older black men. And we've offered high blood pressure screening and counseling to each and every one of them. Thanks to Denny Moe and the myriad other barbers and community leaders who shared the vision of opportunity and empowerment to make a difference in their communities, we've been able to not only lower blood pressure in our participants, but we've also been able to impact other health indicators. So what do you see? What is your barbershop? Where is that place for you where people who are affected by a unique problem can meet a unique solution? When you find that place, see the opportunity. Thank you.
